title: A Generall Rule to Teche Euery Man That Is Willynge for to Lerne, to Serve a Lorde or Mayster in Euery Thyng to His Plesure
summary: The repository artifact is a local CME-submodule representation of University of Michigan item RuleServeLd, produced in 1999 by scanning and ScanWorx OCR from R. W. Chambers's contribution to EETS Original Series 148 (1914). Chambers transcribed the complete three-section household-service tract from British Library Add MS 37969, fols. 2r-8r. U-M retains the component title page and printed text at pp. 11-17 but omits Chambers's introduction, notes, and glossary; print italics marking abbreviation expansions become silent. A complete scan of the 1914 composite book is public through Internet Archive. No verified public complete manuscript facsimile was found in the exact current British Library catalog or its 10 October 2025 digitised-manuscript list; the manuscript remains consultable onsite and photography can be ordered subject to service review and terms.
review_notes: The cataloged work is the one Middle English tract at Add MS 37969 fols. 2r-8r, with three integral rubricated sections and one final explicit; it is not three independently attested works and not a Chambers-created selection.
The publication-set filename and repository work ID are RuleServLd, while the XML IDNO, DIV1 NODE values, and U-M handle use RuleServeLd. The discrepancy is preserved rather than normalized away.

The XML source-description note Derived from olim Seton, olim Pennant (whereabouts unknown) is inherited from the Franciscan portion of the composite 1914 carrier. Chambers identifies Add MS 37969 as the courtesy tract's witness, so no Pennant or Seton provenance edge is attached to this work.

The XML declares Latin only, but direct inspection finds Middle English throughout the cataloged body except the one-word rubric marker Nota. Latin medical recipes on fol. 8v are outside the work; Latin is retained in this graph only because the broader witness and composite carrier contain it.
The repository XML has 25 P elements in total, but only 20 are in BODY; the other 5 occur in HEADER. The three BODY divisions contain 18, 1, and 1 paragraphs.
Talaton, Devon, is associated with separate flyleaf wood memoranda, not with a demonstrated composition or copying place for the tract.
The absence finding for a public complete manuscript facsimile is dated 2026-07-15 and does not claim that the manuscript has never been photographed or digitized.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for RuleServLd
entities.description: Repository XML supplied through the pinned CME submodule. The filename uses RuleServLd, while the embedded U-M item identifier is RuleServeLd.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item RuleServeLd
entities.description: A 1999 University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative electronic text created by scanning Chambers's printed component and performing OCR with ScanWorx. It encodes the Middle English title page and three-section tract, not Chambers's full apparatus, the whole 1914 book, or the complete manuscript.
notes: The XML PROFILEDESC declares only Latin, although the body is Middle English apart from the rubric marker Nota.
The three DIV1 NODE values are RuleServeLd:1, RuleServeLd:2, and RuleServeLd:3.
The generic source-description note linking the text to the former Seton and Pennant manuscript is misaligned carrier metadata and does not identify this work's witness.

entities.label: Chambers 1914 edition of A Generall Rule
alternate_labels: A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules
entities.description: R. W. Chambers's opening component of a composite EETS volume. His courtesy-book component comprises a title page, introduction (pp. 3-8), the complete three-section text (pp. 11-17), notes (pp. 18-22), and a glossary beginning at p. 125. Walter W. Seton edited the two following Franciscan components, not this tract.

bibliographic.citation: A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book, ed. R. W. Chambers, and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules, ed. Walter W. Seton, Early English Text Society, Original Series 148 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., and Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, 1914).
bibliographic.title: A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 148
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., and Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, for the Early English Text Society

notes: The XML title Fifteenth-century courtesy book. Selections reflects electronic cataloging and does not make Chambers's complete tract an edited selection.
The former Seton and Pennant witness described in the carrier belongs to a Franciscan component edited by Seton, not to Chambers's Add MS 37969 tract.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 37969
entities.description: A late-fifteenth-century paper manuscript containing the household-service tract at fols. 2r-8r. Separate material comprises wood memoranda partly concerning Talaton on fol. 1, English and Latin medical recipes on fol. 8v, and a vellum former-binding fragment with fifteenth-century accounts.

holding.notes: The current catalog describes the work as a single Generall Rule at fols. 2r-8r and distinguishes adjacent memoranda and recipes.

physical_description.support: Paper, except for a vellum former-binding fragment
physical_description.extent: i + 8 folios; the cataloged work occupies fols. 2r-8r
notes: The sole work colophon is Explicit a good techyng at fol. 8r.
The current catalog records English as the manuscript language and Latin script; English and Latin recipes on fol. 8v are outside the work.
Talaton appears in separate wood memoranda and is not recorded as the manuscript's production place.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of EETS Original Series 148
entities.description: A complete 150-page digital scan of the composite 1914 volume from the University of Toronto Kelly Library copy. It is a facsimile of the printed edition, not of Add MS 37969.

date_statements.label: PDF derivative metadata date
date_statements.notes: Date recorded in the downloaded PDF metadata; not necessarily the original scanning date.

entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, EPUB, and page-image derivatives

entities.label: British Library digitised manuscripts and archives shelfmark list, 10 October 2025
entities.description: A complete provider list of publicly digitised manuscripts and archives current to 10 October 2025. Add MS 37969 is absent; the list is negative access evidence, not a textual source.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright
entities.description: The April 2026 revision of Copyright Office duration guidance, used only for this manifest's United States rights assessment of the 1914 edition.

agents.name: Walter W. Seton
notes: Editor of the two Franciscan components in the shared 1914 carrier, not of RuleServLd.

scope.notes: The filename loses the internal identifier's second e; the XML header, title, structure, and U-M handle otherwise identify the source item.

subject.description: Electronic title page and three-section Middle English tract

object.description: Chambers's component title page and edited text

scope.notes: The encoding omits Chambers's introduction at pp. 3-8, notes at pp. 18-22, and glossary beginning at p. 125.
The relation covers all three consecutively printed sections and their final explicit, but excludes flyleaf memoranda and fol. 8v medical recipes.

object.description: Complete composite 1914 volume containing Chambers's courtesy-book component

subject.description: Repository work and linked access analysis

object.description: Complete British Library digitised-manuscript shelfmark list

scope.notes: This is a negative access-evidence dependency, not a textual derivation edge.

subject.description: Repository work and linked rights analysis

scope.notes: This is an evidentiary rights-analysis dependency, not a textual derivation edge.

access.access_method: Read the checked-out CME submodule path
Use the U-M CME collection browse entry and stable item handle
notes: The exact item routes presented an automated Cloudflare challenge during this review, while the official collection browse page remained public and listed the item.

access.access_method: Internet Archive item page and direct PDF

local_copies.label: Complete 1914 EETS Original Series 148 edition PDF

work_portion.label: Chambers's A Generall Rule component
locators: PDF pp. 23-29 / printed pp. 11-17: complete edited work
PDF p. 143 / printed p. 125: glossary begins
notes: The U-M encoding retains the title page and printed text pp. 11-17, but not the introduction, notes, or glossary.
The cached PDF is complete for the composite printed edition; it is not a manuscript facsimile.

access.format: PDF, OCR text, EPUB, and page images

access.access_method: Direct PDF from the U.S. Copyright Office

local_copies.label: Complete British Library digitised-manuscript shelfmark list updated 10 October 2025
notes: The complete 261-page provider list contains no Add MS 37969 entry.

access.access_method: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue record
Obtain a valid British Library Reader Pass, request Add MS 37969 through the online form, and consult it in the London manuscripts reading room subject to condition and repository rules

No public complete page-image object was verified: the exact catalog has a blank Digitised Content field, Add MS 37969 is absent from the complete 10 October 2025 digitised-manuscript list, and tested legacy viewer and IIIF candidates did not resolve to the manuscript
notes: This dated negative search does not establish that no institutional photographs or unpublished surrogates exist.

access.access_method: Request color photography of the complete physical object by shelfmark: former-binding fragment f. i and fols. 1-8, both sides, plus covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, and edges where offered; associate the cataloged work specifically with fols. 2r-8r
access.format: Item-specific digital photography subject to service assessment
notes: The service photographs rare manuscripts rather than placing originals on a scanner; availability, format, completeness, schedule, and quotation require item-specific confirmation.
Obtain written reuse permission before publishing supplied files or placing them in the Git repository.

rights.component: The medieval text and Chambers's 1914 transcription, introduction, notes, and glossary
rights.basis: The edition was first published in 1914, before 1 January 1931; the U.S. Copyright Office's April 2026 duration guidance states that U.S.-published works from before that date are public domain, and U-M currently applies No Copyright-United States to CME materials.

rights.component: Materials presented in the U-M CME collection and RuleServeLd item
rights.basis: The current U-M CME collection page states that collection materials are public domain in the United States and applies No Copyright-United States.
notes: The 1999 embedded availability notice permits personal, research, and teaching copies but forbids redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers; whether the current statement supersedes that wording is unresolved.

rights.component: The local U-M-derived XML markup and transcription
rights.basis: The underlying 1914 edition and current U-M collection are public domain in the United States, but the checked-in XML embeds an express redistribution restriction.

rights.component: Internet Archive's digital scan and generated derivatives
rights.basis: The scanned 1914 edition is public domain in the United States, but the reviewed Internet Archive item metadata supplies no item-specific rights or license field for scan derivatives.

rights.component: Modern photographs or digital files supplied by British Library Imaging Services
rights.basis: The imaging-service terms require written permission before supplied files are copied, reproduced, published, or otherwise reused; any permission and fees are separate from delivery of reference files.
notes: The public-domain status of the medieval text does not determine the contractual terms for modern supplied images.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Chambers's component title page, introduction, edited tract, notes, and glossary
editorial_practices.representation: The electronic item retains the component title page and complete edited tract but omits the introduction, notes, and glossary.
editorial_practices.description: The U-M item is a work-level selection from Chambers's larger scholarly component and the still larger composite EETS carrier.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Abbreviation expansions shown by italics in Chambers's printed edition
editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters are retained but the distinguishing italics are omitted, making the expansions silent.
editorial_practices.description: The embedded U-M editorial declaration expressly records the loss of print italics for expansions.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/RuleServLd.xml, header, editorial declaration, title page, and full text.
evidence.summary: Supplies the filename/internal-identifier discrepancy, 1999 U-M identity and ScanWorx production statement, 1914 source, Add MS 37969 title-page statement, omissions, silent expansion practice, embedded provider terms, three sections, folio and page milestones, and selected Middle English text. Its former Seton/Pennant source note is inherited and misaligned.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection and browse listing for RuleServeLd.
evidence.locator: Collection access and rights statement; browse entry for Generall rule
evidence.summary: The current collection reports 289 publicly accessible items, applies Public Domain / No Copyright-United States, and lists RuleServeLd with Add MS 37969, Chambers, and EETS OS 148.
notes: The exact item and resolver routes presented an automated Cloudflare challenge during this review; the official browse listing remained accessible.

evidence.citation: R. W. Chambers and Walter W. Seton, eds., A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules, EETS Original Series 148 (London, 1914), Internet Archive courtesybook00unknuoft.
evidence.locator: Contents; Chambers's component title page; introduction pp. 3-8; text pp. 11-17; notes pp. 18-22; glossary beginning p. 125; Seton's separate Franciscan components
evidence.summary: The inspected complete scan establishes Chambers's responsibility and Add MS 37969 as his source, his singular treatment of the Generall Rule as one tract, the continuously printed three-section text and sole explicit, editorial framing, notes and glossary, and the distinct Seton-edited Franciscan portions to which the former Pennant/Seton note belongs.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item courtesybook00unknuoft and item metadata.
evidence.summary: Provides a retrievable 150-page scan and derivatives of the University of Toronto Kelly Library copy; metadata names scanner uoft1 and supplies no item-specific rights or license field.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, A Generall Rule, Add MS 37969, record 032-002054449.
evidence.locator: Work description, foliation, colophon, date, language, physical description, access, and Digitised Content field
evidence.summary: Identifies the extant late-fifteenth-century witness and one Generall Rule at fols. 2r-8r; includes the fish rule and household officers in the unitary description; records Explicit a good techyng; distinguishes fol. 1 memoranda and fol. 8v recipes; and provides physical-request access while showing no Digitised Content link.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitised manuscripts and archives shelfmark list, updated 10 October 2025.
evidence.locator: Complete 261-page shelfmark list; no Add MS 37969 entry
evidence.summary: The complete provider list of then-public digitised manuscripts does not include Add MS 37969, corroborating the exact catalog's blank Digitised Content field.
notes: Absence from the dated public list is not proof that no private, internal, or later surrogate exists.

evidence.citation: British Library, Archives and Manuscripts item request form and Add MS 37969 catalog access guidance.
evidence.summary: Provides the current online request route and states that a valid or recently expired Reader Pass is required for consultation in London.

Provides an item-specific paid digitisation route, requests shelfmark and folio or page details, explains that rare manuscripts are photographed rather than scanned, and delivers files electronically subject to feasibility and quotation.

evidence.citation: British Library, Terms and conditions for imaging services, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: States that supplied files may not be copied, reproduced, published, or otherwise reused without written permission and that separate permission and fees may apply; it also gives an indicative single-page service timetable rather than guaranteeing a whole-codex order.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.locator: p. 2, works already under statutory protection before 1978
evidence.summary: States that U.S.-published works from before 1 January 1931 are public domain and describes the maximum term for other pre-1978 statutory copyrights; together with U-M's current statement, this supports the U.S. assessment for the 1914 edition.

open_questions.question: Does U-M's current No Copyright-United States statement supersede the 1999 XML restriction against redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers?
next_steps: Obtain a written interpretation from U-M before redistributing the XML or a close derivative.
Continue to distinguish copyright status from provider-contract status.

open_questions.question: Does the British Library hold an existing complete digital or microfilm surrogate of Add MS 37969, and what item-specific access and reuse terms would apply?
next_steps: Ask the British Library by exact shelfmark about existing unpublished surrogates, conservation limits, complete-object photography, delivery format, fees, and item-specific reuse permission.
If ordering new photography, request f. i and fols. 1-8 on both sides plus covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, and edges where offered; associate the work specifically with fols. 2r-8r.
Do not treat the public 1914 book scan as a manuscript facsimile.
